title: hedyscepe canterburyana
intro: Hedyscepe canterburyana, also known as Kawa Kawa, is a plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Annonaceae. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine to treat various ailments, and its medicinal properties include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reduced stress, improved sleep, and relief from anxiety and pain. Herbal preparations of Kawa Kawa include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
